title: eriogonum scalare
intro: Eriogonum scalare, also known as Scouringglove, is a species of flowering plant in the buckwheat family Polygonaceae, native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. Traditionally, the plant's roots and leaves have been used in herbal medicine for their medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antibacterial compounds. The health benefits of Eriogonum scalare are thought to include wound healing, skin care, and the treatment of various infections, due to its antimicrobial properties. Eriogonum scalare can be prepared for medicinal use through infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
